reviews.author: Marc Kenneth Bathan
reviews.text: This is very beautiful! The boat ride itself is already phenomenal. Although, as a solo traveller, I waited for someone to share the boat with to lessen my expenses. Fortunately, I have met a tour company and decided to join their boat. The cave is very beautiful. i can't count how many "wow" I have said in this place. This has been the highlight of my trip

reviews.author: Lisa Beaudry
reviews.text: Feb 27th 2026. A tamer experience than Paradise cave but so worth it. The boat ride and our two young female rowers showed that indomitable Vietnamese spirit and strength. We queued at the ticket office and met up with other tourists from 3 different countries which made our total for 2 people 445 V Dong for what was about a 2 hr trip. Leaving as 1 of the last boats of the day( 3:20) I think quickened their pace and the gift shop at the cave entrance was closing up as we left so we didn't even have to ," Exit through the gift shop" like sooo many tourist experiences do.

reviews.author: J M
reviews.text: Incredible cave, a must see! Entering into the cave via the boat ride is something like a dream, I wished it would last forever, though it felt like a nice amount of time, then they let you off the boat for a walking dry section of the cave and there are nice viewing and picture taking areas built, you might even see the cute little bats. Wear good shoes because of getting in and out of the boat and of course the ground us uneven and slippery, remember it is a cave. I have been to many caves because I love caves and this one is special, such a majestic experience!

reviews.author: Loz Dale
reviews.text: Visited by boat about 10 minutes upstreat from deapart point at town, the boat entered the cave via the river , most impressive & we traveled upstream inside the cave for approximately 800m as an estimate, the cave lighting was excellent highlighting the vast number of Stalagmites and Stalactites, the cave was surprising dry, as I had expected dropping water from the forming Stalactites. The boat was operated by 2 men & we had about 9 passengers, once inside the cave the top of the boat slid back, for be better views & photos. The boat dropped is off in the cave & we were able to experience it's features via well constructed wooden walkways while capturing that perfect photo. The whole experience took about 2 hrs & it was well worth it.

reviews.author: Alexis S
reviews.text: I loved these caves! The boat trip was a lot of fun! You get the see the divide between the Catholic and Buddhist sides of the village. The caves are much bigger than you would expect and you get to see them mostly by boat. There is a short 20 min boardwalk as well. It is definitely worth the visit. We did the two cave tour. The second cave is worth visiting because it is a little more rustic and there are bats. It is a bit of a hike up stairs so it is a bit more difficult to visit. One of the people we shared the boat with struggled with the hike. When we were there we were luck that it was empty. There was very heavy rain on the day we went. You can stay dry though, they have covers on the boats and there is no rain in the cave. You might get a bit wet on the hike to the second cave so a poncho is a good idea if there is heavy rain.

reviews.author: Measured Merit
reviews.text: Amazing colors, mystical vibes with inside cave river and ferns. Jaw-dropping endurance of the young woman who rowed the boat of 12 passengers all bigger and taller than her. Unforgettable experience. A big tip is only a tip of my admiration for a moving experience.

reviews.author: Sascha Hopf
reviews.text: An incredible experience! While the front part of Phong Nha Cave is beautiful with the standard boat tours, the kayak tour is where the real magic happens. ​Why choose the kayak tour? ​Escape the crowds: You go much deeper into the cave than the standard boats. ​Pure Atmosphere: Experience total silence and darkness in the inner chambers. ​Adventure: Includes a fun and challenging climbing section. ​Pro Tip: This is a more active tour. You need to be relatively fit and sure-footed. It’s an authentic adventure and highly recommended for anyone looking for more than just a standard sightseeing trip!

reviews.author: Aidan & Marybeth
reviews.text: Very beautiful cave to look around. We booked this as apart of a day trip and would recommend checking it out 🙂 As apart of the tour we got a boat ride through the cave, and was then able to walk through the cave on the way out. We also did some research and if you wanted you could walk (if based in the main town) to this boat collection point and pay for a boat (probably cheaper than a full tour, by you get no guide)

reviews.author: Geoff Holland
reviews.text: Loved it, albeit a relatively small cave. Being able to cruise right in was a good experience. Learning about the geology and formation by tectonics plates colliding was so interesting and then to see the plates.... I wish I was young enough to canoe all the way to the end.

reviews.author: Timmy Chan
reviews.text: This is one of the best caves I ever visited. It is incredibly beautiful n stunning. Best of all just sit in the boat n enjoy the view. After the boat tour of the cave, we were let off near the entrance of the cave to walk out ourselves. The best n most stunning formations are at this walking path as we walk out. Then board the boat back to the tourism centre on town.

reviews.author: Chris Clemens
reviews.text: Even with limited access due to flooding, Phong Nha Caves were absolutely worth the trip. The parts we could explore were stunning—massive formations, cool river breezes, and that sense of awe you only get in places like this. The boat ride to the cave was beautiful on its own—peaceful river views, lush greenery on both sides, and a great way to settle into the experience. Sure, the water levels meant we couldn’t see everything, but what we did experience was unforgettable. If you’re in the area, don’t let the conditions stop you. Still 100% not a place you want to miss.

reviews.author: Kevin Nuyttens
reviews.text: The caves are beautiful! Absolutely worth the trip even in rainy conditions. Just go to the Phong Nha Tourist Center, you don't have to book a tour with a tour operator. If you are fit, I would highly recommend to pay a little extra and get a ticket for the Tien Son cave as well. It's right next to Phong Nha cave but you have to climb A LOT of stairs. The climb was worth it though, you get a beautiful cave that feels a lot more 'adventurous' to explore and we were the only ones there. We paid 1.210.000 for the two of us. It's a lot of money but the biggest cost is the mandatory boattrip. You can share the cost with others if you want to (and are lucky, for us nobody else was there). Entrance Phong Nha Cave: 150.000 pp Entrance Tien Son Cave: 80.000 pp Boattrip: 700.000 + 50.000 for Tien Son (don't know why they charge extra because it's the same location, maybe for waiting time for the driver) Still cheaper than going with a tour operator.

reviews.author: Monali Sahu
reviews.text: Paradise cave is a beautiful secret of nature , must visit if in Vietnam . We visited dark cave - pitch dark cave where the you walk in barefoot over the mud to reach the mud bath area , an awesome experience with your lives one . We booked it through Klook and the experience was great with our guide- the king .

reviews.author: Kody on the Road
reviews.text: As touristy as it gets! Only advice would be to come with a big group of 12 to make the boat fee as cheap as possible. It’s 700k for the boat. Other than that it’s truly amazing.

reviews.author: Gphuot
reviews.text: 150k/ ticket + 700k for boat. If you go alone Will expensive. But if Share boat cost with group then will be better. bOat capacity 12 poeple. Leave A tips to Boat Rider It Will be Help coz hard Job to Padel inside the Cave 1.2 kms if you are 12 poeple it will very Heard work for them.

reviews.author: Jonathan Weaver
reviews.text: Walking portion is exceptional Boat portion is chaos. Boats block view of each other and it is ridiculous that you have to return on same boat as you arrive given the number of people in separate groups

reviews.author: Nathan Tuck
reviews.text: Went to the dock and we had 4 other people join us for the boat ride( which made it cheaper) headed up to the cave and it was definitely off season. Not many people around. Was a great cave and well worth the 150 000 dong. Great lighting inside the cave.

reviews.author: Nir rov_ink
reviews.text: Really fun experience, The boat took us to the cave about 20-40 min in the water was really amazing view , the cave was beautiful, but smaller than the paradise cave, highly recommend if u love to be on the boat, good instructor told us everything about the cave and the history, The cost for this is between 400-500 depends where u book :)

reviews.author: trung le
reviews.text: Good.. if you into rocks and natures.. photo dropping people i captured 2.21.2026

reviews.author: Zinnia Xu
reviews.text: We came after a few days of heavy rain so we could not take the boat in as far as it normally goes, but we did get to walk around in the cave and see the cave formations.

reviews.author: Sadie Klingele
reviews.text: Really beautiful place to visit. Its very tourist-oriented so it gets quite tightly packed with the boats inside the cave, but even so, its pretty well organized. They also set it up so you can get off and walk around in the cave. You can either kayak or take a boat (private or shared). If you take a boat, tip your drivers well, they're incredible!

reviews.author: Damon Nelson
reviews.text: These caves are gorgeous, I've never been able to get so close to formations while I was in the states!

reviews.author: Damien McGillivray
reviews.text: Found another group to share a boat with, actually 3 groups of people who just met at the ticketing area. Boat cost was shared and we all saved. Cave was cool and an experience to not forget. We did the two cave journey and enjoyed it a lot. Fun was had all round by us and our new friends from UK, Sweden and Italy. We did this system before paradise cave as to be not underwhelmed.

reviews.author: BRIAN ANTHONY
reviews.text: We went on the kayaking tour and walked maybe 1-2 k in the cave. Saw many interesting formations and swimming in cave lake. Tour guides were very nice. Lunch included.... shrimp, chicken, potato, cucumber, corn and more. Arranged trip through Hotel

reviews.author: Terry Angell
reviews.text: Very enjoyable boat trip into the cave and then you walk in to an incredible cathedral that goes on and on with amazing formations. Personally I found this cave topped Paradise Cave, just!

reviews.author: John Winter
reviews.text: Once again, Phong Nha Cave did not disappoint. The journey starts with a scenic 20-minute boat ride along the river surrounded by dramatic limestone cliffs and thick jungle. Due to recent upstream rainfall in Laos, the water level was too high for boats to go deep into the cave, so we disembarked at the entrance and explored on foot. The cave itself is enormous, with massive chambers and breathtaking limestone formations lit up beautifully to showcase their natural detail. The walkways are well-designed, making access inside easy and safe even for casual visitors. From delicate stalactites to towering rock curtains, the cave’s scale and beauty are awe-inspiring. I’d recommend allowing 90–110 to fully walk-through without feeling rushed. If you’re in Phong Nha, this is an absolute must-visit—rain or shine, it’s a geological wonder you won’t forget.

reviews.author: Sandrine Demers-Telmosse
reviews.text: Very beautiful - don’t go with a tour go on your own :)

reviews.author: Bixi Trixi
reviews.text: It was a great visit; riding a boat to the caves, and walking around inside the caves among enormous dripstone formations. It was magnificent!

reviews.author: Lívia Havlíčková
reviews.text: Beautiful I didnt know caves like this exist outside movies, it looks like aliens and celestial beings live on the walls 🤯♥️ i reccomend the boat trip u can buy at main station, you dont need expensive tour its very amazing experience with the boat, you also can walk around the Cave with this Boat trip

reviews.author: Aditya Raj Singh Gour
reviews.text: They do not allow boats to go far away inside the cave, so you can experience only a small portion of the cave. You will get to see both stalactites and stalagmite formations, and boat is the only way to go inside the cave. Once inside we can take a small walk through the dry portion of the cave The journey to cave from the boat sailing in the river, between the mountains is quite spectacular, apart from that if you just want to experience caves then better to skip this one and just go for paradise cave.
